Article.ArticleTitle: At least 10 minutes of seated rest is required for stabilisation of blood-based hydration biomarkers.

Grant.GrantID: National Institute for Health and Care Research
Grant.Agency: National Institute for Health and Care Research

MedlineCitation.CoiStatement: Declarations. Conflict of interest: LJJ is part of the National Institute for Health and Care Research’s Leicester Biomedical Research Centre, which is a partnership between University Hospitals of Leicester NHS Trust, Loughborough University, and the University of Leicester. This report is independent research by the National Institute for Health Research. MPF is funded by the National Institute for Health and Care Research (NIHR) Applied Research Collaboration East Midlands (ARC EM). The views expressed in this publication are those of the authors and not necessarily those of the NIHR, NHS, or the Department of Health. LJJ has current/previous funding from Entrinsic Beverage Company LLC, Entrinsic Bioscience LLC, Herbalife Europe Ltd, Bridge Farm Nurseries, Decathlon SE, PepsiCo Inc., Volac International; has performed consultancy for PepsiCo Inc. and Lucozade, Ribena Suntory; and has received conference fees from PepsiCo Inc. and Danone Nutricia. In all cases, monies have been paid to LJJs institution and not directly to LJJ. SAM has current/previous funding from Entrinsic Beverage Company LLP and Herbalife Europe Ltd and has received consultancy fees from Unilever. SNR was supported by external research funding awarded to LMH from Biopolis S.L. The authors declare no other conflict of interest.

Reference.Citation: Cheuvront SN, Kenefick RW (2014) Dehydration: physiology, assessment, and performance effects. Compr Physiol 4:257–285. https://doi.org/10.1002/cphy.c130017
Corney RA, Sunderland C, James LJ (2015) The effect of hydration status on appetite and energy intake. J Sports Sci 33:761–768. https://doi.org/10.1080/02640414.2014.962578
Dill DB, Costill DL (1974) Calculation of percentage changes in volumes of blood, plasma, and red cells in dehydration. J Appl Physiol 37(2):247–8. https://doi.org/10.1152/jappl.1974.37.2.247
Fawcett JK, Wynn V (1960) Effects of posture on plasma volume and some blood constituents. J Clin Pathol 13:304–310. https://doi.org/10.1136/jcp.13.4.304

Funnell MP, Embleton D, Morris T et al (2023) Exercise-induced hypohydration impairs 3 km treadmill-running performance in temperate conditions. J Sports Sci 41:1171–1178. https://doi.org/10.1080/02640414.2023.2259728
Hagan RD, Diaz FJ, Horvath SM (1978) Plasma volume changes with movement to supine and standing positions. J Appl Physiol 45:414–417. https://doi.org/10.1152/jappl.1978.45.3.414
Harrison MH (1985) Effects on thermal stress and exercise on blood volume in humans. Physiol Rev 65:149–209. https://doi.org/10.1152/physrev.1985.65.1.149
James LJ, Moss J, Henry J et al (2017) Hypohydration impairs endurance performance: a blinded study. Physiol Rep. https://doi.org/10.14814/phy2.13315

Kargotich S, Goodman C, Keast D, Morton AR (1998) The influence of Exercise-Induced plasma volume changes on the interpretation of biochemical parameters used for monitoring exercise, training and sport. Sports Med 26:101–117. https://doi.org/10.2165/00007256-199826020-00004
Liddle L, Monaghan C, Burleigh MC et al (2018) Changes in body posture alter plasma nitrite but not nitrate concentration in humans. Nitric Oxide 72:59–65. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.niox.2017.11.008

Lippi G, Salvagno GL, Lima-Oliveira G et al (2015) Postural change during venous blood collection is a major source of bias in clinical chemistry testing. Clin Chim Acta 440:164–168. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.cca.2014.11.024

Ritz P, Vol S, Berrut G, Tack I, Arnaud MJ, Tichet J (2008) Influence of gender and body composition on hydration and body water spaces. Clin Nutr 27(5):740–746. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.clnu.2008.07.010

Shirreffs SM, Maughan RJ (1994) The effect of posture change on blood volume, serum potassium and whole body electrical impedance. Eur J Appl Physiol Occup Physiol 69(5):461–463. https://doi.org/10.1007/BF00865413
Siebenmann C, Keiser S, Robach P, Lundby C (2017) CORP: the assessment of total hemoglobin mass by carbon monoxide rebreathing. J Appl 123(3):645–654. https://doi.org/10.1152/japplphysiol.00185.2017
Thompson WO, Thompson PK, Dailey ME (1928) The effect of posture upon the composition and volume of the blood in man 1. J Clin Invest 5:573–604. https://doi.org/10.1172/JCI100179

Wickham KA, McCarthy DG, Spriet LL, Cheung SS (2021) Sex differences in the physiological responses to exercise-induced dehydration: consequences and mechanisms. J Appl Physiol 131(2):504–510. https://doi.org/10.1152/japplphysiol.00266.2021
Zappe DH, Tankersley CG, Meister TG, Kenney WL (1993) Fluid restriction prior to cycle exercise: effects on plasma volume and plasma proteins. Med Sci Sports Exerc 25:1225–1230
